action.reasoning: Observation: The k-NN classifier with frozen IN21k ViT-B/16 reached a new best of 85.74% average accuracy in iteration 25. Analysis: The current buffer only stores original training features (~45k). By augmenting the buffer with horizontally-flipped training features (~90k total), k-NN gains denser feature-space coverage, which should improve accuracy. Additionally, predict.py currently averages feature vectors before k-NN; switching to prediction-level TTA (averaging k-NN vote scores from original and flipped test views) is more robust. Decision: Rewrite train.py to cache both original and flipped training features, rewrite predict.py for score-level TTA, and rewrite tune_k.py to tune on val with TTA. Run training+tuning, then submit eval.
